description_long: This house consists of two separate multi-story structures connected by a wide, flat paved area. Both buildings feature steeply pitched, reddish-brown gable roofs and large blue-tinted windows, with the front building showcasing a prominent triangular glass facade. The rear building has a more traditional rectangular form with multiple rows of windows and a subtle overhang above the entry level. The exterior walls are primarily light gray with blue window accents and some darker trim, giving a modern yet residential appearance. The paved area between the buildings could serve as a driveway or communal space. The overall design suggests a contemporary suburban or semi-urban residential complex, possibly a duplex or paired homes.
description: A modern two-building house complex with steep gable roofs, large blue windows, and a wide paved area between the structures. The design features light gray walls, reddish-brown roofs, and a prominent glass entry facade.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main building with a large triangular glass facade and the rear building with rows of blue windows and a gable roof.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the separation between the two buildings and highlights the extensive paved area connecting them.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the back of the main building with a wide roof and glass facade, and the rear building's window arrangement.
description_view_3: Opposite side view emphasizes the angular rooflines and the spatial relationship between the two structures.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with large glass panels
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry features a large triangular glass facade, no visible porch, and a wide paved approach.
